sections.paragraph:
Ἑζεκίας, ου, ὁ (freq. Ἐ-) Hezekiah, in the genealogy of Jesus Mt 1:9f (1 Ch 3:13; 4 Km 18:1ff; 20:1ff; Is 1:1; 38:1ff; TestSol, AscIs, Joseph.; Just., D. 33, 1 al.); Lk 3:23ff D (here the gen. is Ἑζεκία).

freq. freq. = frequent(ly)

TestSol TestSol = Testament of Solomon, I–III a.d.—List 2

AscIs AscIs = Ascension of Isaiah—List 2

Joseph Joseph This abbr. used when no specific texts are cited (s. Jos.); I a.d.—List 5

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

gen. gen. = genitive

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 276
